medications.indication_rationale: First-line for cutaneous LP; apply thinly to active lesions.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply twice daily to active lesions (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Short course for a severe generalised flare to break the itch cycle; taper and avoid repeated courses.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g., 25 mg daily for 5-7 days (as per source note; per prescriber)
contraindications_in_this_case: Screen for infection/diabetes; short course only

medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g., 25 mg at night (as per source note)

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid scratching/scrubbing the skin (Koebner) and apply the steroid ointment only to active bumps.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces new lesion formation and speeds healing.

patient_education_points.point: This is lichen planus — your immune system is attacking the skin and mouth lining, causing these deeply itchy purple bumps. Ordinary creams fail because the inflammation is deep, so we use a strong steroid ointment and a short steroid course.
Don't scratch — new bumps form along scratch lines. The bumps will flatten but may leave brown marks that fade over months. We'll also check a blood test (including hepatitis C).
If the mouth patches become painful open ulcers stopping you eating/drinking, or you get a widespread blistering rash or skin peeling — seek urgent/emergency care.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response and steroid taper, review blood results (incl. HCV), and plan chronic management / dermatology referral; monitor oral LP.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Erosive oral disease preventing intake, or widespread blistering/skin peeling — urgent/emergency care.

must_include_items: Diagnose lichen planus (six Ps/Wickham striae/oral plaques) and screen for a lichenoid drug eruption/SCAR
Potent topical ± short oral corticosteroid and itch/sleep support
Baseline bloods including hepatitis C serology and dermatology follow-up (incl. oral LP)
errors_of_commission: Relying on OTC anti-itch creams only
Repeated/prolonged systemic steroids without specialist input
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires LP diagnosis + potent topical ± short oral steroid + HCV serology + a SCAR/erosive-oral safety-net.
